items.text: Did your cough come on gradually, and what color is what you're coughing up?
choices: Abrupt onset with rust-colored or purulent sputum
Chronic cough with weight loss or night sweats

items.text: Do you have any sharp chest pain that's worse when you breathe in, and has your fever kept coming back despite antibiotics?
choices: Pleuritic pain with fever persisting despite antibiotics

items.text: How short of breath are you — only with activity, at rest, or so bad you can barely breathe?
Do you have trouble swallowing or reflux, or did you choke or aspirate before this started?
choices: Witnessed aspiration event or impaired consciousness at symptom onset

items.text: Have you been in the hospital in the past three months, or are you currently admitted and on a ventilator?
choices: Currently hospitalized more than 48 hours or mechanically ventilated

items.text: Are you on chronic steroids, do you have HIV, a transplant, or are you on chemotherapy?
Have you been incarcerated, experienced homelessness, traveled somewhere with high rates of tuberculosis, or been around someone with TB?
choices: Incarceration, homelessness, or travel to a tuberculosis-endemic area

items.text: Do you smoke, have COPD, or have a lung condition like bronchiectasis?
choices: Known bronchiectasis or other structural lung disease

items.text: Are you able to manage your daily activities on your own, or do you need help?
choices: Independent with activities of daily living
Needs assistance with activities of daily living

items.text: Do you have heart failure, and have you needed extra pillows or woken up gasping for air?
choices: Known heart failure without acute decompensation symptoms
Known heart failure with orthopnea or paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnea

items.text: Have you traveled recently, had surgery, or do you have cancer or a history of blood clots?
choices: Active malignancy or prior venous thromboembolism

items.text: Have you or your family noticed any new confusion?
Assess overall severity from vital signs.
Auscultate the lungs for focal versus diffuse findings.
choices: Focal crackles, bronchial breath sounds, or egophony

items.text: Percuss the chest for dullness suggesting consolidation or effusion.
choices: Dullness with absent breath sounds suggesting a large effusion

items.text: Assess work of breathing and ability to speak in full sentences.
choices: Normal work of breathing, speaks in full sentences
Unable to speak in full sentences

items.text: Assess mental status.
Assess swallowing safety, dentition, and pooling of secretions when aspiration is a concern.
choices: Normal swallow and gag, good dentition
Poor dentition or pooled oral secretions

items.text: Assess for jugular venous distension, an S3 gallop, or peripheral edema suggesting a cardiac contributor.
choices: Jugular venous distension or S3 gallop
Peripheral edema with jugular venous distension

items.text: Assess skin and lip color for cyanosis.
Assess chest wall expansion for symmetry and tracheal position.
Assess body habitus and nutritional status.
